fields.description:
## Context / Problem

rebar's reusable review system separates four concerns (critical finder → generous verifier →
deterministic decision → non-prescriptive coach), and advertises that a **project** may extend
code-review with its own criteria via `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`, exactly as plan-review
already allows (registry story 5065; ADRs 0015/0016/0017; contract in `docs/llm-framework.md`).
Two gaps block that promise:

1. **The generic seam is broken.** A project's overlay merges, but a net-new LLM-backed project
   code-review criterion is then silently dropped at five runtime points — it is never fanned into
   Round A/B, never carries a logical id, never consulted through repository-aware Pass-3 routing,
   and is rejected by the eval surface and the CLI (root cause proven on superseded bug 8a4b:
   `criterion_prompt_id` hardcodes the plan-review prefix `ids.py:31,42`; `CodeReviewBatchRunner`
   iterates only static built-ins `batch_runner.py:49,79`; Pass 3 uses the packaged routing map
   `workflow_ops.py:419,427`; `_code_review_prompt_id`/`calibrate_criterion` exclude project ids
   `eval_solver.py:187-201`, `eval.py:416-420`; the CLI admits only plan-review ids
   `_llm_eval_commands.py:143-155`).
2. **The extension surface is unproven.** No project criterion actually dogfoods the four-phase
   boundary contract, so boundary drift (severity/disposition/fix-instructions leaking into the
   finder, etc.) goes uncaught.

This epic is the single umbrella for both, superseding **jira-reb-1001** (the prior umbrella),
**story 2c14** (the generic-seam story), and **bug 8a4b** (the seam defect). It fixes the seam as a
sequenced, single-concern stack, then authors and dogfoods the first project code-review criterion
on top of the now-working seam.

## Success Criteria

- A project code-review criterion activated in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` executes **once**
  through Round A/B, carries its logical `project.<name>` id on every finding, has its project
  threshold/blocking/nit-suppression govern the deterministic Pass 3, resolves through the
  eval/calibration path, and is admitted by the `criteria eval` CLI (rejected as ambiguous only
  when active in both gates). Delivered by seam children fc72/761e/58ef/8153/41e2.
- An overlay-absent repo behaves **byte-identically** to today; the 13 built-in overlays are
  unchanged (parity). Scope is the **LLM-tier** four-pass finder path; DET-tier criteria are out
  of scope.
- The `project.review-phase-boundaries` criterion and its balanced eval corpus exist (child 225a)
  and are dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review (child 9447). The dogfooding *outcome* depends on a
  live Gerrit review event external to this repo/CI, so it is **[operator-attested]** per ADR 0043
  and verified by child 9447's own completion — not by an epic-level command.
- The runtime contract is documented in `docs/llm-framework.md` (child 19dd).
- jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b are superseded by this epic and closed.

## Decomposition

Eight children. The six **seam** children land as an ordered Gerrit stack, each single-concern on
a **disjoint** production-file set, each growing one RED→GREEN slice of the shared test file
`tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` so every stacked change lands green. The
gate-keyed resolver is first because the others consume it; downstream children reference its
new symbol in forward/usage tense with a `[rebar:<id>]` citation over a direct `depends_on` edge.
The two **feature** children (adopted from the superseded jira-reb-1001) sit on top of the working
seam.

Seam stack (dependency order):
1. **Gate-key the criterion-id resolver** (`criteria/ids.py`) — foundation. — `fc72`
2. **Round-A fan-in + logical-id attribution** (`code_review/batch_runner.py`,
   `workflow/gate_dispatch.py`) — depends on 1. — `761e`
3. **Pass-3 effective routing** (`code_review/workflow_ops.py`) — depends on 2. — `58ef`
4. **Eval-path admission** (`evals/eval_solver.py`, `evals/eval.py`) — depends on 1. — `8153`
5. **CLI admission** (`_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`) — depends on 4. — `41e2`
6. **Docs contract** (`docs/llm-framework.md`) — depends on 5. — `19dd`

Feature children (on top of the seam):
7. **Author `project.review-phase-boundaries` + eval corpus** — depends on 5 (eval/CLI surface). —
   `225a`
8. **Dogfood the criterion in advisory Gerrit review** — depends on 7. — `9447`

No two seam children touch the same production file; child 3 is the only consumer of the
already-existing `registry.effective_routing` (story 5065), so no child re-implements routing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All eight children exist under this epic in the dependency order above, and each seam child
      (1–6) passes `rebar review-plan <child>` with no blocking finding; verified by
      `rebar list --parent 119f-63e3-34b7-4f96` showing eight children and a PASS attestation on
      each seam child.
- [ ] Each root-cause seam point is covered by exactly one seam child with no file overlap;
      verified by cross-checking each seam child's `## Scope` against the five drop points named
      above (one child per point, disjoint production files).
- [ ] The epic supersedes jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b, and all three are closed; verified by
      `rebar show 119f-63e3-34b7-4f96` listing three `supersedes` edges and
      `rebar show <each>` reporting `status=closed`.
- [ ] The two feature children (225a author, 9447 dogfood) are present and dependency-ordered on
      the seam; the dogfooding outcome is tracked as [operator-attested] on child 9447 (ADR 0043),
      not re-verified here; confirmed by `rebar show 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d` depending on 225a.
